Negative clues: high packet loss/jitter dominant, interface congestion with drops. Hypotheses: Queueing delay due to congestion; Suboptimal routing or long path.

distinguishing_clues: TCP retransmits or application timeouts increasing
negative_clues: queue discards due to congestion (tail drop)
physical link errors (CRC) dominant
interface is UP
link is active
render_template: Elevated packet loss detected ({packet_loss_percent}%) on interface {interface} or path. Top hypothesis: {top_hypothesis} (score={prior}).

hypotheses.description: Congestion causing random or tail drops

retrieval_text: Category: performance Intent: Elevated packet loss. Packet loss, packet drop, loss rate. Congestion drops, physical errors, TCP retransmits high.
reranker_text: Intent performance.packet_loss. Summary: Elevated packet loss rate impacting throughput and applications. Distinguishing clues: packet loss percent >1%, TCP retransmits increasing. Negative clues: queue discards only, physical CRC dominant. Hypotheses: Congestion causing drops; Physical layer corruption. The physical interface is UP and operational. This is a performance drop issue, NOT a physical link down.

good average latency but high variation
negative_clues: consistent high latency without variation
pure packet loss without delay variation

retrieval_text: Category: performance Intent: High jitter delay variation. High jitter, jitter variation, delay variation, voip quality poor. Congestion jitter, route instability.
reranker_text: Intent performance.jitter_high. Summary: High jitter (packet delay variation) affecting real-time applications. Distinguishing clues: jitter_ms >30ms, variable latency with good average. Negative clues: consistent latency, pure packet loss. Hypotheses: Variable queueing due to congestion; Unstable routing.

retrieval_text: Category: performance Intent: Microburst detected. Microburst, traffic burst, short burst drop. Brief high PPS causing drops despite low average utilization.
reranker_text: Intent performance.microburst_detected. Summary: Short-duration microbursts causing packet drops. Distinguishing clues: brief spikes with drops, low average utilization. Negative clues: sustained congestion, steady loss. Hypotheses: Insufficient buffer; Bursty application traffic.

retrieval_text: Category: performance Intent: QoS misclassification. Qos misclassification, qos not working, wrong class. Critical traffic in default queue, marking errors.
reranker_text: Intent performance.qos_misclassification. Summary: QoS policy failing to classify or prioritize traffic correctly. Distinguishing clues: critical traffic in best-effort queue, unexpected queue drops. Negative clues: no QoS applied, general congestion. Hypotheses: Incorrect DSCP marking; Policy or class-map misconfig.

retrieval_text: Category: performance Intent: Bufferbloat excessive buffering. Buffer bloat, bufferbloat, high latency low loss. Large standing queues, poor interactive apps.
reranker_text: Intent performance.buffer_bloat. Summary: Excessive buffering leading to high latency with low loss. Distinguishing clues: high latency + very low packet loss, large queues. Negative clues: high loss with low latency, queue drops. Hypotheses: Oversized buffers without AQM; No active queue management.

negative_clues: symmetric path with general congestion

retrieval_text: Category: performance Intent: Asymmetric routing. Asymmetric routing, return path different, asym routing. Stateful firewall drops, one-way performance issues.
reranker_text: Intent performance.asymmetric_routing. Summary: Asymmetric forward/return paths causing performance degradation. Distinguishing clues: different paths observed, stateful device issues. Negative clues: symmetric congestion, routing loop. Hypotheses: ECMP imbalance; Policy-based routing. TCP state mismatch due to asymmetric paths (e.g. firewall drops). The physical interfaces are perfectly healthy without errors.

retrieval_text: Category: performance Intent: MTU mismatch fragmentation. MTU mismatch, fragmentation, df bit drop, pmtud failure. Large packets failing, ICMP needed.
reranker_text: Intent performance.mtu_fragmentation. Summary: MTU mismatch leading to fragmentation or blackholing. Distinguishing clues: high fragmentation or PMTUD issues, large packets fail. Negative clues: uniform packet loss, congestion. Hypotheses: Path MTU mismatch; PMTUD blackhole. Packet size exceeds MTU. Jumbo frames failing. The interface itself is healthy without physical errors.

retrieval_text: Category: performance Intent: General slow application performance. Application slow, slow app, poor user experience, throughput low. Composite network degradation.
reranker_text: Intent performance.application_slow. Summary: Overall slow application performance over the network. Distinguishing clues: multiple metrics degraded, user-reported slowness. Negative clues: single metric dominant, server resource issue. Hypotheses: Combination of network issues; Server-network handoff problem. Application layer latency and throughput issues. NOT caused by physical layer interface CRC or input errors.
